title: Neomammillaria aurihamata
intro: Neomammillaria aurihamata, also known as golden-hairy mammillaria, is a species of cactus native to Mexico. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria aurihamata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Neomammillaria aurihamata is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. The genus Neomammillaria is now generally considered to be a synonym of Mammillaria, so Neomammillaria aurihamata would be classified as Mammillaria aurihamata. Mammillaria is a genus of small cacti with globular stems and are typically found in Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. This genus is further classified into several species, with Mammillaria aurihamata being one of them. It belongs to the order Caryophyllales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants.
